name: plexus-showcase
description: Plexus: an interop mesh that discovers the capabilities each flagship produces and consumes, then wires them together.

steps.title: What Plexus is
steps.command: echo plexus - a cross-flagship interop mesh that finds and wires real capability edges
steps.narration: Plexus is the connective tissue. It looks across the flagships, finds which one produces a capability another one needs, and draws the wiring between them.

steps.title: The capability surface
steps.narration: Discover the mesh, list producer and consumer pairs, plan the upstream pipeline that feeds an organ, or route a path from one tool to another. It turns a pile of separate tools into a graph you can reason about.

steps.title: The live mesh
steps.narration: And here is the mesh itself: the organs, and the real edges between them, each one pointing at the exact function that carries the capability across. Mneme feeding a thesis into Crucible, Gather feeding a digest. The whole spine, discovered rather than declared.
